Ramanarayanam

# Ramanarayanam

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Mar 21, 2026

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

4

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

Ramanarayanam in Vizianagaram is truly one of the most unique Rama temples in Andhra Pradesh. What makes it remarkable is not just its architectural grandeur but also the vision behind it. Built by Narayanam Narasimhamurthy, a successful businessman and philanthropist heading the NCS Group, the temple reflects both devotion and generosity.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!F-vA!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F2afae2c0-094e-4859-bbd5-b779726d73e3_320x480.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!F-vA!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F2afae2c0-094e-4859-bbd5-b779726d73e3_320x480.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!xgHp!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffcaef38e-0728-45d3-9457-13ffb1230ae6_296x168.png "Image")

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!loLv!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7b89c179-6afd-4745-98ff-6d8772513f97_1600x960.jpeg)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!loLv!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F7b89c179-6afd-4745-98ff-6d8772513f97_1600x960.jpeg "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Narayanam Narasimhamurthy’s life is a luminous example of how prosperity can be transformed into Dharma. Born into a family business rooted in ports, he expanded its reach by establishing two bulk liquid storage terminals at Kakinada and Karwar. Yet, instead of hoarding wealth, he chose to channel it into service—building temples, nurturing communities, and uplifting lives.

His devotion found expression in the **Ramanarayanam temple at Vizianagaram**, a bow-shaped marvel dedicated to Lord Rama, and in the **Krishna temple at Gokulam, Kakinada**. But his vision extended far beyond architecture. He built **Padmavathi Nilayam at Tirumala**, later handed over to TTD, donated land in Vizianagaram for a **Kalyanamandapam**, and supported an **old age home**. His generosity flowed into TTD’s **Annadana scheme**, ensuring that countless devotees were fed, and he even arranged for milk to be supplied to pilgrims waiting in queues at Tirumala.

His compassion embraced all beings,donating cows to TTD’s Goshala, and his philanthropy touched the future by educating poor children in Vizianagaram. Each act was not a gesture of charity alone, but a deliberate offering to Dharma, a way of weaving service into the fabric of everyday life.

Indeed, Narayanam Narasimhamurthy Garu’s crowning achievement was the creation of **Ramanarayanam** at Vizianagaram,a massive theme park cum temple dedicated to Lord Rama and the Ramayana. Spread across 15 acres, it was inaugurated in 2014 and has since become one of the most celebrated attractions in Andhra Pradesh.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!dqjH!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F1fddb08b-6fc5-4d18-a1f5-568043493fcf_945x612.jpeg)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!dqjH!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F1fddb08b-6fc5-4d18-a1f5-568043493fcf_945x612.jpeg "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!rWQB!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F3608dc55-968f-4a81-be0a-57f5363e67c6_338x450.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!dlFv!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F57c8b894-0bec-4195-aa39-1504659ebb83_296x168.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!dlFv!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F57c8b894-0bec-4195-aa39-1504659ebb83_296x168.png "Image")

The design itself is symbolic and striking. The entire complex is shaped like a **bow and arrow**, echoing Lord Rama’s weapon, and housed within a two-storey structure. At the tip of the arrow stands a **majestic statue of Hanuman**, radiating devotion and strength. Surrounding this centerpiece are lush gardens, trees, and landscaped pathways that create a tranquil atmosphere, inviting visitors to reflect and immerse themselves in the story of Rama.

Walking through Ramanarayanam is like journeying through the Ramayana itself, each corner narrates episodes of the epic, blending artistry, devotion, and education. It is not only a temple but also a cultural landmark, where spirituality meets storytelling. Today, it stands as one of the main attractions in Andhra Pradesh, drawing pilgrims, tourists, and seekers alike.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!8n91!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F0450e66c-9e20-49ea-bc9f-98a3e1ef6f16_640x480.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!8n91!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F0450e66c-9e20-49ea-bc9f-98a3e1ef6f16_640x480.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

The **Ramanarayanam Temple complex** is not only architecturally striking but also botanically and spiritually rich. One of its most fascinating sections is the series of sacred groves. **Nakshatra Vanam, Narayana Vanam, Raasi Vanam, Navagraha Vanam, Vinayaka Vanam, Saptarishi Vanam, Panchavati Vanam, and Pancha Bhoota Vanam**. Each grove is planted with trees and plants associated with cosmic forces, deities, and elements, creating a living tapestry of faith and nature.

This thoughtful integration of sacred botany makes the temple a place where visitors can connect with the Ramayana not just through sculptures and stories, but also through the natural world. The **two-tier gallery** adds another dimension: its sculptures depict scenes from the Ramayana in sequence, so that walking along feels like stepping back into the era of the great epic. Every episode, from Rama’s birth to the final coronation,is etched in stone, making the entire Ramayana accessible in one immersive journey.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!TFB-!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F75485dae-e168-4186-8d2a-6773d363cfcb_800x600.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!TFB-!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F75485dae-e168-4186-8d2a-6773d363cfcb_800x600.png "Image")

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!7tsk!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8313539b-2ff5-4991-a2b9-ba783ab08c1b_1600x846.jpeg "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

At night, the **Ramanarayanam Temple complex** transforms into an enchanting wonderland. The bow-and-arrow shaped structure, already majestic by day, glows under carefully designed lighting that highlights its contours and sacred spaces. Colorful fountains dance in rhythm, casting reflections across the landscaped gardens and sacred groves,Nakshatra Vanam, Raasi Vanam, Navagraha Vanam, and more,turning them into shimmering sanctuaries.

The towering statue of Hanuman at the tip of the arrow becomes even more awe-inspiring under the night sky, illuminated against the backdrop of trees and pathways. As visitors walk through the two-tier gallery, the Ramayana sculptures seem to come alive in the interplay of shadow and light, immersing them in the epic’s timeless aura.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!32Gy!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F96e74b20-85f7-4198-8226-3d8ca5188576_550x413.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!32Gy!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F96e74b20-85f7-4198-8226-3d8ca5188576_550x413.png "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Fxao!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6789ea8c-6a9d-419c-b792-5594bcb54c42_296x168.png "Image")

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!tFxO!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8f2b90d0-493e-4015-b86d-9cc63a5b1c1f_1200x900.jpeg)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!tFxO!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F8f2b90d0-493e-4015-b86d-9cc63a5b1c1f_1200x900.jpeg "Image")

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

This is one of those places which is a must visit. The entire concept, the design, the architecture is a wonder to be experienced and seen.

Ramanarayanam Temple can be easily reached from Vizag by road and Vizianagaram itself is well connected by rail, road. The temple complex is visited by many in Andhra Pradesh, Odisha, but if you are making a trip to Vizag, make sure this is part of your intinerary.

Narayanam Narasimhamurthy Garu passed away in 2007, but his son Narayanam Srinivas has carried on his father’s work well, and the Ramanaraynam Temple will forever be a legacy to cherish. Respect to this great man, who needs to be known to all.

It is people like him who keep Sanathana Dharma alive, chanelling their wealth towards Dharmic causes, serving selflessly without any publicity. Respect to such great souls.

To those who are interested you can check out more on Ramanarayanam at their official web site [here.](https://t.co/8NmoWhvGm5)
